rule_name: In-Wall Strapping By Others Note Validation

view_matching_logic.Section: Check only Section views that show the standard in-wall strapping structure: a narrow veritical or horizontal  rectangular backing/strap plate near the wall/cabinet interface with X-cross lines inside and a bolt/fastener/block detail. Do not check every Section. Omit Sections showing only countertop, HSS support, sink/knee clearance, toe kick, floor datum, freestanding support, or countertop support unless the complete in-wall strapping symbol is visible.

geometry_logic.boundary_detection: The rule is activated only when the exact Section visibly contains the standard in-wall strapping symbol/structure: a narrow veritical or horizontal rectangular backing/strap plate near the wall/cabinet interface, with X-shaped cross lines inside the rectangle and a bolt/fastener/block detail associated with the symbol.Do not activate from Section type, cabinet presence, wall line, gypsum board, cabinet back, shim space, leader arrow, or note text alone. Geometry_applicable must be true only when this physical X-cross rectangular strapping symbol is visible in the same Section crop. Each Section must be validated independently; do not reuse an in-wall strapping note or geometry from another Section on the same sheet.
wall_interaction_conditions: Section shows the standard in-wall strapping symbol: narrow veritical or horizontal rectangular plate with X-shaped cross lines inside.
The symbol is located near the wall/cabinet interface or inside/adjacent to the wall line.
A small bolt, fastener, or block-like detail may be present near or through the X-cross rectangular plate.
Leader arrow or note may point to the symbol, but arrow/text alone is not enough.
Do not activate for plain cabinet back, wall finish, gypsum board, shim space, or wall line unless the X-cross rectangular strapping symbol is visible.
Do not activate for countertop, curtain wall, HSS support, sink clearance, toe kick, floor datum, door, or freestanding base cabinet sections.
geometry_logic.expected_boundary_region: The expected region is the exact Section area where the standard in-wall strapping symbol is visible: narrow veritical or horizontal rectangular plate with X-cross lines and a bolt/fastener/block detail, located near the wall/cabinet interface. The detected note must be inside or immediately associated with that same view crop and same view_id.

pass_condition: The exact detected view is a Section that shows physical wall/cabinet in-wall strapping or physical X-cross rectangular in-wall strapping symbol.
The in-wall strapping condition is active in that same view and not inferred from a different Section.
The required note or accepted variant is visibly present in that same view near the wall/cabinet interface.
Detected note evidence, detected_text, and coordinates are non-empty and belong to the same view_id/crop.
Confidence score is greater than or equal to 75.
fail_condition: The exact Section clearly shows physical in-wall strapping/physical X-cross rectangular in-wall strapping symbol requiring the note.
The required note is missing from that same view while the applicable wall/cabinet interface is clearly visible.
Failure is not allowed based on Section type alone; the physical X-cross rectangular in-wall strapping symbol must be active.
review_required_condition: The Section crop is unclear, clipped, rotated, or too low-resolution to determine whether physical in-wall strapping is shown.
The cabinet-to-wall condition is visible but it is unclear whether support is in-wall strapping by others or another support type.
The note text is partially readable or ambiguous.
Multiple Section views are present and the detected note cannot be confidently associated with the exact view.
A centerline strapping note is visible but physical in-wall strapping by others is not clearly shown.
omit_condition: The detected view is not a Section.
The Section does not show the complete standard in-wall strapping symbol: a narrow veritical or horizontal rectangular plate with X-cross lines and a bolt/fastener/block detail.

The Section is a countertop section, countertop with bracket, countertop with equipment, countertop support, HSS support, curtain wall interface, sink clearance, toe kick, finished floor, or similar condition where the standard in-wall strapping symbol is not visible.
The note appears in another Section but not in this exact view.

reasoning.description: Explain whether the exact Section contains the complete standard in-wall strapping symbol: narrow veritical or horizontal rectangular plate with X-cross lines and a bolt/fastener/block detail, whether the note was found in that same view, and why similar Sections or countertop/support Sections without that symbol are omitted.

view_results.view_type: Detected actual view title from drawing
